_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: GET /openapi.json returns HTTP 500 — create_app().openapi() raises Pydantic ForwardRef('Response') error; app-wide OpenAPI schema generation is broken

steps.description: Backend: reproduce the Pydantic ForwardRef('Response') error in-process (`uv run python -c 'from dashboard.app import create_app; create_app().openapi()'`), capture the exact traceback (which model / field / route is in the chain), bisect via `git grep -n 'Response' dashboard/routers/ dashboard/app.py orch/` to locate the offending annotation, and apply the SMALLEST possible fix (likely 1-3 LOC): add a missing non-TYPE_CHECKING import, drop a string-quoted return annotation, set `response_class=Response` on a route decorator, or add a `model_rebuild()` after the relevant import. Verify `app.openapi()` returns a valid dict and `client.get('/openapi.json')` returns 200 via TestClient (targeted run of the new test file in S03 is not the deliverable of S01 — S01 only needs to confirm the schema generator no longer raises). Do NOT refactor adjacent code; do NOT widen scope; if other latent OpenAPI issues surface during the bisect, note them in the report and let the operator file follow-ups.

Per-agent review of S01: correct root cause located via reproducible traceback (not guessed); fix is the smallest possible change (1-3 LOC); diff stays inside scope.allowed_paths; no adjacent refactors smuggled in; report includes the captured ForwardRef traceback and a one-line statement of which of the three candidate root-causes (route signature / response model / __future__ annotations) was the actual fault.

Tests: (a) CREATE tests/dashboard/test_openapi_schema.py with two regression tests — test_i_00111_openapi_endpoint_returns_valid_schema (uses the dashboard `client` fixture, asserts status==200, asserts schema has 'openapi' starting with '3.', non-empty 'info.title', and len(paths)>0) and test_i_00111_app_openapi_callable_returns_dict (calls `create_app().openapi()` in-process, asserts dict + non-empty paths). (b) MODIFY tests/dashboard/test_schemathesis_contract.py — remove the `_json_api_openapi()` closure and the `monkeypatch.setattr(app, 'openapi', _json_api_openapi, raising=False)` line at the former lines 163-173, switch `contract_schema` to `schemathesis.openapi.from_asgi('/openapi.json', contract_app)` against the real app schema, and update the module docstring at lines 21-30 to note that I-00111 fixed the underlying ForwardRef bug and the workaround is no longer needed. (c) Targeted verification only: `uv run pytest tests/dashboard/test_openapi_schema.py -v --no-cov` AND `uv run pytest tests/dashboard/test_schemathesis_contract.py::test_json_api_paths_exist_in_schema -v --no-cov -m contract_fuzz` — do NOT call make test-integration or make test-unit. Semantic asserts only (specific values, never just shape).

Per-agent review of S03: both regression tests assert SEMANTIC correctness (specific status code 200, specific OpenAPI version prefix '3.', specific 'paths non-empty' contract) — never just shape (`'paths' in schema` alone is BAD); the workaround removal is complete (the `_json_api_openapi` closure and the monkeypatch line are both gone, not just one); the contract_schema fixture loads via from_asgi against the real app; the module docstring is updated; no full-suite test runs in the step.

Global cross-agent review of S01..S04: AC1 (GET /openapi.json returns valid 3.x schema with non-empty paths), AC2 (both regression tests green), and AC3 (schemathesis workaround removed, contract_schema loads via from_asgi against real app) all verified end-to-end. Scope check: `git diff origin/main --name-only` is limited to files inside scope.allowed_paths. Full unit + integration suites run as part of the review.
QV: Assertion scanner (forbid new vacuous tests)
QV: Integration tests — runs the new OpenAPI schema regression tests
Self-assessment of the just-completed item via the iw-item-analyze skill
